description: Fix 6 integration connect/disconnect bugs: persist disconnect for session-token services, handleFocus preConnectStatusRef check, polling/focus race guard, handleMessage unconditional toast, disconnect success toast, and optimistic UI revert on failure.
steps: 1. Backend: Add explicitly_disconnected flag to /disconnect endpoint, /status endpoint, and /callback endpoint
2. Frontend: Add preConnectStatusRef check to handleFocus effect
3. Frontend: Add processingConnectionRef to guard against polling/focus race
4. Frontend: Add preConnectStatusRef check to handleMessage
5. Frontend: Change connectionToast to store full message string, add disconnect success toast
6. Frontend: Revert optimistic UI on disconnect failure by re-fetching status
acceptance_criteria: Disconnect persists even when session token has service scopes
handleFocus uses preConnectStatusRef to avoid false positive toasts
Polling and handleFocus cannot race (processingConnectionRef guard)
handleMessage only shows toast for newly connected services
Failed disconnect reverts optimistic UI by re-fetching actual status
Toast renders the full message string directly (not hardcoded 'connected successfully!')
python3 -m py_compile passes for backend integrations.py
findings: All 6 bugs fixed and verified. Backend py_compile, frontend lint, and frontend build all pass. The explicitly_disconnected flag is stored alongside the service entry in Firestore rather than deleting it. Toast rendering now uses the full message string from connectionToast state. The processingConnectionRef prevents concurrent fetch calls from handleFocus and the polling interval.
